channel.title: Caesar's Commentaries — Family Reading Edition on Dual Classics
channel.description: Recent content in Caesar's Commentaries — Family Reading Edition on Dual Classics

item.title: Chapter 1: The Three Parts of Gaul
item.description: Caesar begins by explaining the lands and peoples of Gaul.

item.title: Chapter 2: The Helvetii Prepare to Leave
item.description: Orgetorix urges the Helvetii to leave their homeland and seek power in Gaul.

item.title: Chapter 3: Caesar Reaches Geneva
item.description: The Helvetii burn their homes, gather at the Rhone, and ask Caesar for passage.

item.title: Chapter 4: Caesar Blocks the Road
item.description: Caesar builds a barrier from Lake Geneva to Mount Jura and refuses the Helvetii passage.

item.title: Chapter 5: A Dangerous New Route
item.description: The Helvetii gain passage through the Sequani, and Caesar hurries to gather more legions.

item.title: Chapter 6: The River Saone
item.description: Caesar attacks the Tigurini at the Saone, then answers Divico’s warning.

item.title: Chapter 7: Five Miles Behind
item.description: Caesar follows the Helvetii closely while his army begins to run short of grain.

item.title: Chapter 8: Liscus Names the Trouble
item.description: Liscus reveals that powerful men among the Aedui are helping the enemy.

item.title: Chapter 9: Divitiacus Pleads for His Brother
item.description: Caesar considers punishing Dumnorix, but Divitiacus begs him for mercy.

item.title: Chapter 10: The Mountain Mistake
item.description: Caesar tries to trap the Helvetii near a mountain, but a frightened scout gives a false report.

item.title: Chapter 11: Turning Toward Bibracte
item.description: Caesar turns aside for grain, and the Helvetii think the Romans are retreating.

item.title: Chapter 12: The Battle on the Hill
item.description: The Romans and Helvetii fight a long battle around the hill and the wagons.

item.title: Chapter 13: Surrender and Return
item.description: The Helvetii surrender, and Caesar orders them back to their homeland.
